II. \(b\gt c\)

In a school, \(55\%\) of the students are boys. It is given that \(60\%\) of the boys and \(30\%\) of the girls live in Kowloon. Find the probability that a randomly selected student from the school is a girl who lives in Kowloon.

The pie charts below show the expenditures of Albert and Betty in a month.

Which of the following must be true?
options: In that month, the expenditure of Albert is less than that of Betty.
In that month, the percentage of rent in the expenditure of Albert is the same as that of Betty.
In that month, the expenditure on rent of Albert is the same as that of Betty.
In that month, the expenditure on food of Albert is twice that of Betty.
